answer:
The Pythagorean major scale of F-sharp consists of the notes F♯, G♯, A♯, B, C♯, D♯, E♯. In Pythagorean tuning, an apotome is the chromatic semitone, which is the difference between a note and its sharpened version. The two apotome pitch names in this scale are B♯ (raised from B) and F♯ (the tonic, but in context the sharpened version of F natural), making B the correct choice.

\boxed{B}
